name: Peryton
description: The Peryton is a fascinating creature from Greek mythology, described as a winged stag with the head, forelegs, and antlers of a stag and the wings of a bird. Legend states that they originated from Atlantis, specifically from people who had been cursed or transformed. These creatures are often depicted as casting human shadows until shortly before their own death, a chilling omen that adds to their mystique. Perytons are said to harbor a deep hatred for humans, particularly those who caused their original transformation or downfall. They are known for their aggressive nature and tendency to attack travelers, often plunging down from great heights with deadly force. The Peryton's origins and motivations are steeped in tragedy and vengeance, making them a unique and compelling figure in the realm of mythological beasts. Their eerie shadows and aggressive behavior have solidified their place in folklore as creatures to be feared and respected.
subtitle: A winged stag with a dark origin.
appearance: The Peryton possesses the head, neck, and forelegs of a stag, complete with antlers. Its body is covered in reddish-brown fur, and it has large, feathered wings similar to those of an eagle or other large bird of prey. It is described as being roughly the size of a large deer or elk.
habitat: Mountainous regions, dense forests, and ruins, particularly those associated with ancient civilizations or lost cities like Atlantis.
behavior: Perytons are typically solitary creatures, though they may gather in small groups during mating season. They are fiercely territorial and will aggressively defend their hunting grounds. They are known to attack humans on sight, often targeting travelers or those who venture too close to their lairs. Their behavior is driven by a deep-seated hatred and a desire for revenge.

keyMyths.title: The Origin of the Peryton
keyMyths.description: The Peryton is said to originate from Atlantis, born from a curse placed upon humans or the souls of those who died violently. This curse transformed them into these winged stag-like creatures, forever bound to haunt the world and seek vengeance for their lost lives. This is why they hold such animosity towards humans.
keyMyths.source: Jorge Luis Borges, Book of Imaginary Beings

keyMyths.title: The Shadow of the Peryton
keyMyths.description: A unique characteristic of the Peryton is its shadow, which is said to resemble that of a human until the moment of its death. This unsettling phenomenon is interpreted as a reminder of their human origins and a constant torment, fueling their rage and aggression. This also acts as a warning to those who are being hunted.

keyMyths.title: Peryton Attacks
keyMyths.description: Stories tell of Perytons attacking travelers and isolated settlements, diving down from the sky with incredible speed and force. Their antlers and venomous bite make them formidable opponents, and their hatred for humans makes them relentless in their attacks. The attacks are often described as sudden and brutal, leaving little chance for survival.
keyMyths.source: Various online mythologies and folklore websites

sources.author: Jorge Luis Borges
sources.description: A compilation of mythological creatures, including a brief description of the Peryton and its origins.

sources.author: Rosemary Ellen Guiley
sources.work: The Encyclopedia of Demons and Demonology
sources.description: While not strictly a demon, the Peryton's aggressive nature and association with dark magic sometimes leads to its inclusion in demonological texts.
sources.text: While not strictly a demon, the Peryton's aggressive nature and association with dark magic sometimes leads to its inclusion in demonological texts.
